---
title: "Projects"
---

## Clinical Trial Patient Matching (CTPM)

Our flagship research platform for semiautomated prescreening of cancer patients against open clinical trials.

**What it does**

- Ingests structured and unstructured EHR data via the OMOP common data model
- Applies hybrid rules-based and NLP logic to evaluate inclusion/exclusion criteria
- Delivers real-time feasibility assessments and patient-trial match lists
- Scales across trials, cancer types, and health system deployments

**Impact**

Validated on metastatic colorectal cancer trials and extended to 29 oncology studies. Supported by Yale Cancer Center T-TARE and YNHH Innovation Awards.

[Read the 2026 publication →](publications.qmd)

---

## CtrlTrial

[CtrlTrial](https://www.ctrltrial.com/) is a translational platform founded by Dr. Gong to bring AI-driven clinical trial matching from Yale research into health system practice.

**Capabilities**

- Real-time patient prescreening from Epic and integrated clinical systems
- Clinician and trial team notifications when patients become eligible
- Customizable search parameters for trial-specific criteria
- Analytics on enrollment trends and diversity metrics
- API integration with EHR, pathology, laboratory, and CTMS systems

CtrlTrial was developed through Yale Tsai CITY programs and validated in pilot deployments with Yale New Haven Health. It won the **2024 Yale New Haven Health Innovation Award**.

[Visit CtrlTrial →](https://www.ctrltrial.com/)

---

## Eligibility Criteria Visualizer

An NLP framework for structuring and exploring clinical trial eligibility criteria at scale.

**Approach**

1. Unsupervised clustering of semantically similar criteria from trial protocols
2. LLM-based summarization of criteria clusters
3. Interactive visualization of patterns by disease domain and over time

The prototype analyzed **53,000+ oncology trials** from ClinicalTrials.gov, including breast, lung, and GI oncology domains. Open-source tools are available via the [CriteriaVisualizer repository](https://github.com/ctrltrial/CriteriaVisualizer).

Funded by the Yale New Haven Health Innovation Award (2024–2026).

[Read the study protocol →](https://www.researchprotocols.org/2026/1/e86425)

---

## Real-time EHR data integrity

A collaborative project with the Schulz Lab assessing whether real-time EHR extracts meet the quality standards required for clinical research—benchmarking latency, completeness, and accuracy against research-grade datasets.

[Publication →](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41511976/)

---

## Research informatics infrastructure

We contribute to Yale-wide informatics initiatives including:

- Patient recruitment workflows connecting primary care providers through the EMR and patient portal
- Real-time specimen identification for collaborative biobanking
- Computational phenotyping pipelines for precision medicine

These projects build on Dr. Gong's experience at the Yale/YNHH Center for Outcomes Research and Evaluation (CORE).
